codeReview.summary: The cross-process-lifecycle feature implements a comprehensive integration test suite for the complete process lifecycle (fork -> exec -> wait -> exit). The implementation includes 13 well-structured tests that validate all 5 cross-area validation assertions (VAL-CROSS-009 through VAL-CROSS-013). The code follows project conventions, uses proper thread synchronization, and includes thorough documentation. All tests pass successfully.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test file follows the established IOX_TEST framework pattern with proper naming conventions (lifecycle_* prefix for test functions). Uses iox_ prefix for internal functions and IOX_ prefix for macros consistently. All 13 tests properly use IOX_ASSERT* macros from the test harness.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-system/Tests/unit/test_process_lifecycle.c - All test functions use IOX_TEST() macro and assertion macros; helper functions use iox_ naming (iox_waitpid, iox_exit, etc.)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly understood that iOS forbids actual fork() and created tests using simulated process creation via iox_task_alloc(). The create_test_child() helper properly simulates fork behavior by duplicating file descriptors, filesystem context, and signal handlers - which is the correct approach for iXland's thread-based process simulation architecture.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: test_process_lifecycle.c:55-82 create_test_child() helper - correctly calls iox_files_dup(), iox_fs_dup(), iox_sighand_dup() and sets up parent/child relationships

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test implementation correctly uses pthread_mutex_lock/unlock for thread-safe access to shared task structures (parent->children list, task state). This demonstrates proper understanding of the threading model documented in architecture.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: test_process_lifecycle.c:84-92 simulate_child_exit() uses pthread_mutex_lock; multiple tests lock parent->lock before traversing children list

sharedStateObservations.observation: Tests include comprehensive documentation headers that explicitly map each test to the validation assertions it fulfills. This pattern should be encouraged for all cross-area integration tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: test_process_lifecycle.c:95-99 - 'Test: Complete fork/wait lifecycle... Validates: VAL-CROSS-009, VAL-CROSS-011, VAL-CROSS-012' - this pattern repeated for all 13 tests

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered and correctly used the existing test harness (iox_test.h) and test runner infrastructure. The tests integrate properly with the iox-core-tests binary and support filtering by test name pattern ('lifecycle').
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows: './build-macos/ixland-system/iox-core-tests lifecycle' - exitCode: 0, 'All 13 lifecycle tests passed'

summary: The cross-process-lifecycle feature is well-implemented and complete. It adds 13 integration tests in test_process_lifecycle.c that comprehensively validate the fork/exec/wait/exit process lifecycle. All tests pass and fulfill their targeted validation assertions (VAL-CROSS-009 through VAL-CROSS-013). The code follows project conventions, uses proper thread synchronization, and includes excellent documentation. No blocking or non-blocking issues found.
